top_signals.title: Thai scammer's $122M wallet, Japan embraces crypto credit: Asia Express
top_signals.summary: Bitcoin backed mortgages and stablecoin yield are hot topics in Japan right now, as Hyundai experiments with Avalanche for global stablecoin transfers. Asia Express
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: The United States produced more crude oil than any other country in 2025
top_signals.summary: The United States remained the world's largest crude oil producer in 2025, according to our International Energy Statistics database, extending a streak that began in 2018 when the United States overtook Russia to become the world's leading producer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: CHART: Mining vs AI – Nvidia sneezes, sheds five BHPs
top_signals.summary: Nvidia lost a trillion dollars down the back of the couch this quarter and briefly traded like a value stock. The world's 50 biggest miners, all $2.19 trillion of them, have looked like ones for years.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Federal Reserve announces the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy
top_signals.summary: Federal Reserve announces the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Russia's FSB blamed for Poland grid attack as UK and EU impose first joint cyber sanctions
top_signals.summary: The allies blamed Center 16, the FSB’s signals intelligence arm, for acts of attempted cyber sabotage targeting Poland’s energy sector and water treatment facilities, alongside “a wide range of malicious cyber activities with growing severity.”
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. exports of crude oil and petroleum products reached record in April
top_signals.summary: U.S. petroleum exports reached a record in April as disruptions to international crude oil and refined product flows through the Strait of Hormuz increased global demand for U.S. exports. Exports increased to 13.6 million barrels per day (b/d) in April, 15% more than the previous record set in March.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: CLARITY Act gains second law enforcement endorsement before Senate push
top_signals.summary: The Federal Law Enforcement Officers Association expressed support for the CLARITY Act but urged changes to the legislation’s language.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Hackers backdoor Jscrambler npm package with infostealer malware
top_signals.summary: The Jscrambler client-side web security company disclosed that a threat actor published a malicious version of its npm package that has been downloaded almost 1,500 times. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: TeraWulf CEO: 'Not All Megawatts Are Created Equally' in AI Race
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Gondor v1 to let users borrow against their entire Polymarket portfolio
top_signals.summary: Expected to launch in September, v1 will enable users to margin their entire Polymarket portfolios to place additional leveraged bets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, May 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. goods and services trade deficit increased in May 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit increased from $54.6 billion in April (revised) to $77.6 billion in May, as exports decreased and imports increased. The goods deficit increased $23.6 billion in May to $106.5 billion. The services surplus increased $0.6 billion in May to $28.9 billion. Full Text

top_signals.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
top_signals.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.
